decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly and efficiently reanimating powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Griselbrand to overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave to cheat creatures into play from the graveyard. Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy help disrupt the opponent's game plan while cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal allow for explosive plays. Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers provide support and additional threats. The key strategy is to assemble the reanimation combo as quickly as possible to win the game.

The main focus of this deck is to establish control over the game by utilizing land destruction and denial, while also providing a mana advantage through cards like Urza's Saga and Exploration. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Wasteland and Ghost Quarter, while also providing a way to recur key cards with Life from the Loam. The inclusion of Boseiju, Who Endures and Disruptor Flute allows for protection against counterspells and other disruption, while Malevolent Rumble serves as a win condition by dealing damage to the opponent's creatures.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures and mana acceleration in order to quickly and aggressively disrupt their game plan. The deck includes cards like Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer to apply pressure, while also utilizing cards like Chalice of the Void to control the opponent's spells. By playing aggressively and disrupting the opponent's strategy, this deck aims to secure a fast victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on utilizing lands as both control elements and win conditions through combos such as Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. The strategy revolves around ramping with cards like Exploration, Mox Diamond, and Life from the Loam to quickly assemble powerful land combinations. Additionally, the deck contains disruptive elements like Wasteland and Boseiju, Who Endures to disrupt opponents' strategies while maintaining a strong board presence. The deck is designed to control the game through strong land interactions and outvalue opponents with efficient land-based threats.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of powerful creatures like Uro and Barrowgoyf, efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Assassin's Trophy, and counterspells like Force of Will. It also utilizes cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for key pieces and maintain card advantage. Additionally, it includes cards like Wasteland and Daze to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind them out with its versatile threats and disruptive spells.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and aggro creatures like Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent. It utilizes tempo plays with cards like Daze and Wasteland to control the pace of the game while applying pressure with efficient threats. The deck also includes powerful answers like Fatal Push and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to deal with opposing threats. Its strategy revolves around controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy while maintaining a strong offensive presence.

A control deck focused on locking down opponents' plays through the use of artifacts and powerful prison effects. The deck utilizes cards like Urza's Workshop, Trinisphere, and Karn, the Great Creator to restrict the opponent's options while generating advantage through cards like Ugin, the Eye of the Storms and Kozilek's Command. The goal is to establish a strong board presence through artifacts and disrupt the opponent's strategy, ultimately leading to victory through overwhelming power or resource denial.

The key concept of this deck is to apply pressure on the opponent early in the game through a combination of aggressive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Delver of Secrets, backed up by powerful removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Wasteland and Force of Will while maintaining tempo with cards like Daze. By constantly applying pressure and disrupting the opponent's plans, this deck aims to quickly close out games and secure victory.

This Legacy combo deck aims to quickly fill up the graveyard with creatures using cards like Cabal Ritual and Dark Ritual, before casting Dread Return to bring back a creature like Undercity Informer to mill the entire library. With key pieces like Narcomoeba and Thassa's Oracle, the deck can win the game by either milling the opponent out or using Thassa's Oracle's enter the battlefield ability to win the game instantly. Protection spells like Pact of Negation and disruption spells like Duress and Thoughtseize help to keep the combo going smoothly.

The deck focuses on creating a prison-like control strategy using artifacts to lock down the opponent's resources and disrupt their game plan. By generating massive mana with Urza's Workshop and Grim Monolith, the deck can play powerful threats like Ugin, Karn, and Emrakul while also utilizing cards like Trinisphere and The One Ring to restrict the opponent's options. Urza's Saga, Candelabra of Tawnos, and Voltaic Key provide additional utility and flexibility in manipulating artifacts and generating advantage. Overall, the deck aims to slow down the game and overwhelm the opponent with powerful artifacts.

The deck focuses on assembling the Dark Depths combo as quickly as possible using cards like Elvish Reclaimer, Knight of the Reliquary, and Thespian's Stage. The strategy is to use cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Crop Rotation to search for the pieces needed to create the 20/20 Marit Lage token. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending to deal with opposing threats, as well as utility lands like Karakas and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The key is to efficiently ramp and protect the combo to win the game.

The key concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly drop powerful Eldrazi creatures onto the battlefield and overwhelm the opponent with their strength and abilities. By utilizing mana acceleration cards like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, along with Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin to reduce costs, the deck aims to play aggressive threats like Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer early on. Chalice of the Void is used to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while additional support cards like Wastescape Battlemage and Kozilek's Command provide added flexibility and control options.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with aggressive Eldrazi creatures and disrupt their strategy with cards like Chalice of the Void. By utilizing powerful Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Reality Smasher, along with mana ramp from cards like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, you can establish a dominant board presence and dictate the pace of the game. The deck also includes cards like Kozilek's Command for removal and disruption, making it a versatile and threatening aggro deck in the Legacy format.

This lands control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources while accelerating its own mana production and generating value from lands. The deck utilizes cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to create powerful token creatures, while also controlling the board with cards like Wasteland and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale. Exploration and Life from the Loam provide card advantage and mana ramp, allowing the deck to deploy threats quickly. The inclusion of Sphere of Resistance and Boseiju, Who Endures helps to protect key pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck aims to apply early pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Delver of Secrets, while disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. By utilizing cards like Scalding Tarn and Mishra's Bauble to smooth out draws, the deck can maintain a high tempo and keep up the pressure. Removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat help control the board, allowing the deck to close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck aims to win quickly by casting a combination of spells that allow for a win condition without playing any lands. Using fast mana sources, discard spells to disrupt the opponent, and various creatures like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire library, the deck eventually plays Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot by having no cards left in the library. The combo relies on speed and disruption to win before the opponent has a chance to react.
